## Verses (30)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | And Job made answer and said, | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | If only my passion might be measured, and put into the scales against my trouble! | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | For then its weight would be more than the sand of the seas: because of this my words have been uncontrolled. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | For the arrows of the Ruler of all are present with me, and their poison goes deep into my spirit: his army of fears is put in order against me. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Does the ass of the fields give out his voice when he has grass? or does the ox make sounds over his food? | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | Will a man take food which has no taste without salt? or is there any taste in the soft substance of purslain? | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | My soul has no desire for such things, they are as disease in my food. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | If only I might have an answer to my prayer, and God would give me my desire! | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | If only he would be pleased to put an end to me; and would let loose his hand, so that I might be cut off! | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | So I would still have comfort, and I would have joy in the pains of death, for I have not been false to the words of the Holy One. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | Have I strength to go on waiting, or have I any end to be looking forward to? | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | Is my strength the strength of stones, or is my flesh brass? | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | I have no help in myself, and wisdom is completely gone from me. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | He whose heart is shut against his friend has given up the fear of the Ruler of all. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | My friends have been false like a stream, like streams in the valleys which come to an end: | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | Which are dark because of the ice, and the snow falling into them; | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | Under the burning sun they are cut off, and come to nothing because of the heat. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | The camel-trains go out of their way; they go up into the waste and come to destruction. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | The camel-trains of Tema were searching with care, the bands of Sheba were waiting for them: | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | They were put to shame because of their hope; they came and their hope was gone. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | So have you now become to me; you see my sad condition and are in fear. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Did I say, Give me something? or, Make a payment for me out of your wealth? | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | Or, Get me out of the power of my hater? or, Give money so that I may be free from the power of the cruel ones? | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | Give me teaching and I will be quiet; and make me see my error. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | How pleasing are upright words! but what force is there in your arguments? | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | My words may seem wrong to you, but the words of him who has no hope are for the wind. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | Truly, you are such as would give up the child of a dead man to his creditors, and would make a profit out of your friend. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | Now then, let your eyes be turned to me, for truly I will not say what is false to your face. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | Let your minds be changed, and do not have an evil opinion of me; yes, be changed, for my righteousness is still in me. | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
| [30](BBE.md#v30) | Is there evil in my tongue? is not the cause of my trouble clear to me? | [WEB](WEB.md#v30) · [KJV](KJV.md#v30) · [ASV](ASV.md#v30) |
